en.extract:
Ruspoli's turaco (Menelikornis ruspolii), also known as Prince Ruspoli's turaco, is a species of bird in the family Musophagidae.  It is endemic to southern Ethiopia where its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical dry forests.  It is threatened by habitat loss.


Taxonomy

The species was first collected by Eugenio Ruspoli in either 1892 or 1893, but due to his subsequent death, the location and date of this find remain unknown. However, his collection was examined by T. Salvadori, who named the bird in his honor.


Habitat
Prince Ruspoli's Turaco, also known as Tauraco ruspolii, is primarily found in juniper (Juniperus) woodland accompanied by dense evergreen undergrowth near Arero and Wadera. However, it is believed that this elusive bird species has a preference for drier forest edges, acacia-conifer woodland, and mixed broadleaf scrub. It typically inhabits elevations ranging from 1250 to 1860 meters above sea level.


References


External links
 Media related to Tauraco ruspolii at Wikimedia Commons
 Data related to Menelikornis ruspolii at Wikispecies
BirdLife Species Factsheet.

'''Ruspoli's turaco''' ('''''Menelikornis ruspolii'''''), also known as '''Prince Ruspoli's turaco''', is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Musophagidae]].  It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to southern [[Ethiopia]] where its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical dry [[forest]]s.  It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].

==Taxonomy==
[[File:Tauraco ruspolii (id 8907).jpg|thumb|Ruspoli's turaco photographed in the Negele Borena region in Ethiopia]]
[[File:Tauraco ruspolii.jpg|thumb|Ruspoli's turaco photographed in the Negele Borena region in Ethiopia]]
The species was first collected by [[Eugenio Ruspoli]] in either 1892 or 1893, but due to his subsequent death, the location and date of this find remain unknown. However, his collection was examined by T. Salvadori, who named the bird in his honor.<ref>[http://www.selamta.net/birds.htm Endemic Birds of Ethiopia]</ref>

== Habitat ==
Prince Ruspoli's Turaco, also known as Tauraco ruspolii, is primarily found in [[juniper]] (Juniperus) [[woodland]] accompanied by dense evergreen undergrowth near [[Arero]] and [[Wadera, Ethiopia|Wadera]]. However, it is believed that this elusive bird species has a preference for drier forest edges, acacia-conifer woodland, and mixed broadleaf scrub. It typically inhabits elevations ranging from 1250 to 1860 meters above sea level.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Prince Ruspoli's Turaco articles - Encyclopedia of Life |url=https://eol.org/pages/45513999/articles?lang_group=en&resource_id=775 |access-date=2023-10-26 |website=eol.org}}</ref>
